content:
- Final validation is green: make check, make lint, make test, make test-long, and nix flake check all passed on the current host.
- Updated the bug task to status done/passes true and ran .ralph/task_switch.sh. Next is stage everything, commit, and push.

- Added result/result-* to .gitignore so Nix symlink artifacts are not committed.
- Removed the scratch smell task instead of leaving an unresolved sidecar. Next step is git add -A, review staged summary, commit, and push.

- Finished task bug-fix-nix-flake-check-without-docker end-to-end.
- Updated the task file to done/passes true, ran task_switch, committed all staged changes as bcde512, and pushed master to origin.

- Read previous progress, refreshed Ralph summaries, and confirmed only two active failing tasks remain in story-32.
- Verified the actual stack from the repo: Nix-driven Rust workspace plus a vendored Go verify binary, with image outputs defined in flake.nix and no existing .github/workflows directory.
- Read both story-32 task files; task 02 explicitly extends task 01, so next step is to point .ralph/current_task.txt at task 01 unless blocker metadata contradicts that.

- Selected the next task and created .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Chose .ralph/tasks/story-32-github-workflow-multiplatform-image-artifacts/01-task-create-magic-nix-cache-matrix-workflow-and-combine-image-artifacts.md because it is an unblocked failing non-bug task and task 02 explicitly extends it.
- Next agent should execute story-32 task 01, then re-run task selection after its completion because task 02 is the remaining dependent workflow extension.

- Read saved progress and confirmed current task pointer targets story-32 task 01 about the GitHub workflow/image artifacts lane.
- Loaded the tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions for this turn.
- Next I am reading the task file and the improve-code-boundaries skill directory to determine whether this turn is plan-only or execution.

- Read story-32 task 01; it is still not_started with no plan marker, so this turn should produce the required plan file and stop.
- Confirmed this is a workflow/infrastructure task with an explicit TDD exception; verification must be real/manual rather than brittle text-based tests.
- Next I am inspecting the repo's current workflow state, flake image outputs, and related story task context so the plan is specific and executable.

- Confirmed there is no .github directory yet and flake.nix already defines per-system runner-image and verify-image outputs.
- make targets are Nix-backed only, which matches repo rules and means the future execution step must use nix-based validation.
- Next I am checking repo layout and boundary-smell guidance so the plan can include one concrete code-boundary improvement rather than just adding a large inline workflow.

- Verified from the GitHub Marketplace primary source that Magic Nix Cache still documents `DeterminateSystems/magic-nix-cache-action@main`, paired after Nix installation.
- Next I am reading the rest of flake outputs and existing scripts to pin exact build targets and decide which workflow logic should live in repo scripts versus inline YAML.
- Task 02 filename differs from the earlier summary, so I am also checking it for downstream coupling before writing the plan.

- Read story-32 task 02 as downstream context: it assumes task 01 creates the five-way parallel GHCR workflow, then task 02 layers Quay publishing on top.
- Next I am checking existing Ralph plan conventions so I can write the plan file and update the task file with the expected marker/path format before stopping.
- I am also checking the current scripts README because the likely boundary improvement is to move image artifact assembly/inspection out of giant inline YAML into repo-owned CI scripts.

- Found the Ralph convention: task files carry a `<plan>...</plan>` path, and plan files end with a `Plan path:` line plus either `TO BE VERIFIED` or `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next I am reading one previous workflow-heavy plan and a recent bug task/plan pair so I can mirror the exact structure before editing the current task.

- Before writing the plan, I am doing one quick Nix inspection of the image outputs so the execution plan can name the artifact handoff truthfully instead of assuming the dockerTools output shape.
- Then I will create the story-32 task plan file, link it from the task markdown, and stop as required.

- Read the current task and determined this turn had to be planning-only because task 01 had no linked plan yet.
- Gathered current repo context: no .github workflow exists, flake already exports runner-image/verify-image for x86_64-linux and aarch64-linux, and the Nix image outputs are docker-image-*.tar.gz archives suitable for artifact handoff.
- Verified current primary-source docs for Magic Nix Cache usage and GitHub runner labels, then wrote the execution plan at .ralph/tasks/story-32-github-workflow-multiplatform-image-artifacts/01-task-create-magic-nix-cache-matrix-workflow-and-combine-image-artifacts_plans/2026-04-28-magic-nix-cache-matrix-ghcr-plan.md.
- Updated the task file to link that plan.
- The plan explicitly uses the improve-code-boundaries skill by moving multi-arch assembly/publish logic into a repo-owned scripts/ci boundary instead of inline YAML shell soup.
- The plan ends in NOW EXECUTE, so the next turn should execute that plan, run make check/lint/test plus actionlint and hosted GitHub verification, and switch back to TO BE VERIFIED only if the workflow design assumptions prove false.

- Read the saved progress, current task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill instructions.
- Confirmed the previous turn already created the task plan and marked it NOW EXECUTE, so this turn should execute the plan unless design assumptions break.
- Next I am reading the task markdown, linked plan, and improve-code-boundaries skill directory guidance before making any edits.

- Read the task markdown and linked execution plan; this turn is executing the workflow/script design already approved in the task.
- Confirmed the main boundary objective: keep workflow YAML focused on orchestration and move OCI archive assembly/publish logic into a repo-owned script under scripts/ci.
- Next I am inspecting flake image outputs, existing scripts, and git/remote state before writing the workflow and publish script.

- Added the first implementation pass: .github/workflows/publish-images.yml plus scripts/ci/publish-ghcr-multiarch-from-archives.sh, with publish logic moved out of YAML to keep the boundary clean.
- The workflow currently has one nix flake check job, a four-cell image matrix across amd64/arm64 and runner-image/verify-image, and one dependent publish job that logs into GHCR and runs the repo script.
- Next I am running bash syntax checks, actionlint, and reviewing the exact diff before validating the artifact build/load path.

- Local validation found a host assumption bug in my validation command, not in the workflow: this machine is aarch64-linux, so trying to build x86_64-linux image outputs locally failed with explicit Nix platform-mismatch errors.
- actionlint and shell syntax checks were already green before that failure.
- Next I am rerunning the local artifact proof against the actual host system so the archive/load validation stays honest.

- Local validation is now green for the new workflow/script boundary: actionlint, bash syntax, shellcheck, Docker loading of real host-architecture Nix image archives, and a dry-run of the four-artifact publish script contract.
- One validation command had to be corrected after discovering the workstation is aarch64-linux; the final local proof was rerun against the actual host architecture instead of assuming x86_64-linux.
- Next I am running the required repository gates: make check, make lint, and make test.

- The required local repo gates are green after the workflow changes: make check, make lint, and make test all passed.
- At this point the only unresolved acceptance criteria are the hosted GitHub workflow run, log inspection, and GHCR publish verification.
- Next I am reviewing the exact staged changes and then preparing a candidate push so I can inspect the real workflow run through the authenticated GitHub API helper.

- Pushed candidate commit 7143ea6 to origin/master to trigger the real GitHub Actions workflow on a normal push event.
- Next I am using the authenticated GitHub API helper to locate the workflow run for that commit and inspect job ordering, cache behavior, artifact handoff, and GHCR publish evidence.
- If the hosted run fails, I will iterate with follow-up commits before doing the final Ralph/task bookkeeping commit.

- Hosted run 25068613846 proved the five-way parallel job topology and artifact upload path, but its Magic Nix Cache logs surfaced an avoidable non-fatal FlakeHub auth error from the action default.
- I corrected the workflow to set use-flakehub: false on both Magic Nix Cache steps so hosted logs stay clean and do not contain misleading ERROR lines.
- Next I am rerunning local validation and then pushing a second commit; if the first run is still active, that will also prove the workflow does not cancel older runs via concurrency.

- Hosted failure analysis showed nix flake check itself was correct to fail: the x86_64 GitHub runner hit bwrap uid-map permission errors inside the cockroachdbRuntime buildFHSEnv wrapper used by molt-go-test.
- I flattened that boundary in flake.nix by replacing the buildFHSEnv-based Cockroach runtime with a direct autopatched binary package, which should remove the hosted dependency on bubblewrap entirely.
- Next I am validating the exact previously failing test surface locally with nix run .#test-molt before rerunning the full local gates and pushing again.

- The runtime-boundary fix worked locally: COCKROACH_BIN is now a direct autopatched store binary, and nix run .#test-molt passed end-to-end.
- That validates the exact hosted failure path without weakening the flake check surface.
- Next I am rerunning actionlint, local nix flake check, and the required make check/lint/test gates before pushing the second hosted workflow validation commit.

- Pushed follow-up commit df3435b after fixing the hosted nix flake check failure at its real source: the Cockroach runtime wrapper.
- This second hosted run should now validate three things at once: the x86_64 flake-check fix, cleaner Magic Nix Cache logs with use-flakehub disabled, and cache reuse from the first run where available.
- Next I am inspecting the new run and its job logs through the authenticated GitHub API helper.

- Final hosted validation is green on run 25069126637, including the dependent publish job and commit-SHA-only GHCR tags for runner-image and verify-image.
- Updated the task file to completed/passes true with local and hosted evidence, including the intermediate hosted failure and the cockroach runtime boundary fix that removed the bwrap dependency.
- Next I am running task_switch, staging all files including .ralph artifacts, committing with the required task-finished message, and pushing.
